ngl.def: 'Not gonna lie' — flags honesty or a mild confession.

iykyk.def: 'If you know, you know' — signals an inside reference not everyone will get.

fr.def: 'For real' — emphasises agreement or truth. 'fr fr' = doubly so.

tfw.def: 'That feeling when' — sets up a relatable or comedic scenario.

tbh.def: 'To be honest' — prefaces a candid opinion.

wsg.def: 'What's good' — a casual greeting, like 'what's up'.

fwiw.def: 'For what it's worth' — softens an opinion you're offering.

imo.def: 'In my opinion' — flags a personal take. 'imho' adds 'humble'.

smh.def: 'Shaking my head' — expresses disappointment or disbelief.

irl.def: 'In real life' — offline, as opposed to online.

tldr.def: 'Too long; didn't read' — a one-line summary, or an admission you skipped the wall of text.

icl.def: 'I can't lie' — same job as 'ngl': prefaces honesty.

atp.def: 'At this point' — usually introduces a resigned conclusion: 'atp just leave.'

grwm.def: 'Get ready with me' — a video format where a creator talks while doing makeup, hair, or outfits.

ootd.def: 'Outfit of the day' — a post showing what you're wearing.

pov.def: 'Point of view' — labels a video where you see a scenario through someone's eyes; often used loosely for any skit setup.

dni.def: 'Do not interact' — a profile warning telling certain groups to stay away.

oomf.def: 'One of my followers/friends' — talking about someone without naming them.

fyp.def: 'For You Page' — TikTok's algorithmic home feed. 'Made it to my fyp' = the algorithm showed me this.

ong.def: 'On god' — swearing something is true; stronger than 'fr'.

sdiybt.def: 'Start digging in your butt, twin' — an absurdist SpongeBob-derived phrase whose entire point is that it means nothing. Used to confuse or to signal chaotic humour.

goat.def: 'Greatest of all time' — the best ever at something. Often just the goat emoji 🐐.

op.def: 'Original poster' — the person who started the thread. In gaming it means 'overpowered'.

npc.def: 'Non-player character' — acting robotic, predictable, on autopilot; someone with no main-character energy.

fomo.def: 'Fear of missing out' — anxiety that something fun is happening without you.

jomo.def: 'Joy of missing out' — the smug opposite of FOMO.

dm.def: 'Direct message' — a private message. 'Slide into the DMs' = initiate flirty private contact.

rt.def: 'Retweet' — now used in speech to mean 'I agree / same'.

w.def: A 'win'. 'Big W' = great outcome. Opposite of L.

l.def: A 'loss' or failure. 'Take the L' = accept defeat. Opposite of W.

gg.def: 'Good game' — said at the end of a match; also used for 'it's over / done'.

afk.def: 'Away from keyboard' — not at the screen; loosely, absent.

lowkey.def: Slightly, secretly, or 'kind of'. Softens a statement. Opposite of 'highkey'.

highkey.def: Openly, very much. 'Highkey obsessed' = openly obsessed.

unserious.def: Ridiculous, not to be taken seriously — usually said with amused affection.

bet.def: 'Okay / deal / for sure' — an energetic agreement. 'You coming?' 'Bet.'

slay.def: To do something extremely well, or look amazing doing it.

sus.def: Suspicious or shady. Popularised globally by Among Us.

simp.def: Someone over-eager for another person's attention or affection, to their own embarrassment.

stan.def: An obsessive superfan; to be one. From Eminem's song 'Stan'.

ghosting.def: Cutting off all contact with someone, suddenly and without explanation.

situationship.def: A romantic arrangement that's more than friends but refuses to define itself.

the ick.def: A sudden, irreversible feeling of repulsion toward someone you were attracted to, usually triggered by something small.

rent-free.def: 'Living rent-free in my head' — you can't stop thinking about it.

mid.def: Mediocre, overrated, thoroughly average. A dismissive verdict.

based.def: Confidently yourself and indifferent to others' opinions; agreeing hard with a take.

cringe.def: Embarrassing second-hand discomfort, as adjective or noun. The opposite of based.

hits different.def: Feels uniquely better in this context than it normally does.

touch grass.def: 'Log off and go outside' — telling someone they're too online.

main character.def: Acting like the protagonist of the world. 'Main character energy' can be a compliment or a warning.

side eye.def: A look of suspicion or judgement, often just typed or the emoji.

era.def: A self-defined life phase: 'in my villain era', 'in my soft era'.

cooking.def: Doing something impressively well: 'let him cook'. But 'cooked' = doomed, finished.

locked in.def: Fully focused and committed. 'I'm locked in this semester.'

yapping.def: Talking too much, rambling. 'Yapper' = chronic over-talker. Now sometimes affectionate.

ratio.def: When a reply outperforms the post it answers — the crowd sided against the original.

ate.def: Did something exceptionally well. 'Ate and left no crumbs' = flawless.

fell off.def: Declined in quality or relevance from a former peak.

gaslight.def: Manipulating someone into doubting their own reality. Online, often used loosely for any deception.

no cap.def: 'No lie' — emphasising you're telling the truth. 'Cap' = a lie.

it's giving.def: 'It's giving [X]' — it has the vibe or energy of X.

pull up.def: To show up or come through to a place or event.

gng.def: 'Gang' — a friendly term of address, like 'bro' or 'fam'.

rizz.def: Charisma; the ability to charm a romantic interest. Word of the year 2023; now used mostly ironically.

delulu.def: 'Delusional', affectionately — believing something against the odds. 'Delulu is the solulu.'

sigma.def: A 'lone wolf' male archetype — cool, successful, introverted. Now used almost entirely ironically by kids.

skibidi.def: A mood-dependent intensifier from the absurdist Skibidi Toilet series — can mean cool, ridiculous, or worthless depending entirely on context.

yeet.def: To throw something with force; an exclamation of energy. Firmly dated now.

on fleek.def: Perfectly styled or executed. A 2014-15 artifact — using it now is retro at best.

cheugy.def: Off-trend in a millennial way. The word itself has since become cheugy.

mewing.def: Originally a jawline 'technique'; now meme shorthand for staying silent — 'I'm just mewing' is an accepted non-answer.

crash out.def: To lose emotional control spectacularly and often publicly; 'crashout' is the episode or the person. One of the defining terms of 2025-26.

choppelganger.def: 'Chopped' + 'doppelganger' — an unflattering lookalike. The internet's bluntest comparison format.

chopped.def: Unattractive, undesirable, or badly done. 'I'm so chopped today.'

clanker.def: A derogatory term for AI, robots, or people who over-rely on AI. Borrowed from Star Wars' battle-droid insult.

ai slop.def: Low-effort, mass-produced AI-generated content flooding feeds. The defining pejorative of the AI content era.

shrekking.def: Dating someone you're not attracted to in the hope they'll treat you well — settling in advance. Named after Shrek.

i know ball.def: 'I deeply understand this subject and have elite taste.' Confidence claim, originally about sports knowledge.

404 coded.def: Acting absent, empty, or error-like — named after the 'page not found' error. 'He's so 404 coded' = nobody's home upstairs.

canon event.def: An unavoidable formative life experience you just have to let happen — 'don't interfere, it's a canon event.'

beige flag.def: A partner trait that's neither good nor bad, just oddly boring or quirky.

brain rot.def: The mushy-brain feeling from too much absurd internet content — now a beloved genre itself. Oxford's 2024 word of the year.

aura.def: Your social 'cool points'. Gained by being impressive, lost by being cringe. '-1000 aura.'

aura farming.def: Doing things specifically to raise your social cool-factor. Now slightly past peak; often said ironically.

six seven.def: '6-7' — a nonsense audio meme chanted for comedic effect, often with a hand gesture. Asking what it means is missing the point. Beloved by kids, exhausting to everyone else.

looksmaxxing.def: Maximising physical attractiveness through grooming and fitness; can veer into unhealthy territory.

mogging.def: Visibly outshining someone in looks or presence.

sneaky link.def: A secret romantic meetup, or the person you're secretly seeing.

body count.def: The number of people someone has slept with — a crude metric that fuels endless discourse.

thirst trap.def: A deliberately attractive photo posted to harvest attention.

ragebait.def: Content posted purely to provoke angry reactions and drive engagement.

edgelord.def: Someone who performs shock value and dark 'edgy' takes for attention.

doomscrolling.def: Compulsively scrolling bad news long past the point of wellbeing.

micro-drama.def: Ultra-short serialized vertical video soap operas — episodic cliffhanger content built for phones, one of 2026's defining formats.

great meme reset.def: A 2026 movement calling for a return to nostalgic, human-made memes in reaction to AI slop and brain rot fatigue.

nihilistic penguin.def: A penguin filmed in a 2007 documentary marching alone toward the mountains instead of the sea — adopted in 2026 as the mascot of opting out of everything.

kitkat heist.def: Memes about the real 2026 theft of roughly 400,000 KitKat bars in Europe — the internet demanded a documentary about the 'chocolatiest crime'.

i am my perimeter.def: 'I am my perimeter and my perimeter is me' — a streamer's deadpan Rainbow Six Siege line turned all-purpose declaration of being locked in.

💀.def: The skull = 'I'm dead' — something is so funny you've metaphorically died laughing.

😭.def: Rarely actual crying — usually 'this is hilarious' or 'this is too much', same family as 💀.

🧢.def: The cap = a lie. Reply '🧢' to call cap; 'no 🧢' = truth.

💅.def: Nail polish = unbothered, sassy confidence; doing something effortlessly while ignoring drama.

🗿.def: The moai = deadpan, stoic, or ironic 'sigma' energy. Often a reply meaning 'bro.'

✨.def: Sparkles = ironic emphasis: '✨boundaries✨'. Sarcasm marker as much as decoration.

🤡.def: The clown = self-own or calling someone foolish: 'me thinking they'd text back 🤡.'

🐐.def: The goat = GOAT, greatest of all time.

🫡.def: Salute = respectful acknowledgement, 'on it', or mock-solemn duty.

🥀.def: Wilted rose = melodramatic heartbreak or ironic despair; a staple of sad-posting.

👁️👄👁️.def: Wide eyes and lips = stunned, speechless witnessing of chaos.

pageText: ngl the kitkat heist memes are lowkey the best thing on my fyp all week 💀 whoever stole 400k chocolate bars said it's giving canon event, no cap. half the replies are clanker accounts posting ai slop tho, istg the great meme reset can't come soon enough. anyway the nihilistic penguin understood the assignment — he saw the discourse and chose the mountains, and tbh? based. atp I'm just mewing.
examples: bro really crashed out over his choppelganger, -1000 aura, he's so 404 coded 💀
ngl the kitkat heist memes are cooking, no cap that's a canon event fr
she's shrekking him atp, beige flag after beige flag, istg i know ball when i see it
